segments.text: Do you think it's honest to claim you are right before God?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: And you ask, ‘What benefit do I get? What good has it done me by not sinning?’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I'll tell you, and your friends too!
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Just look up at the sky and see. Observe the clouds high above you.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If you sin, how does that harm God?[^238] How do your many sins affect God?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If you do what's right, what good are you doing for him?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: No—your sins only affect people like yourself, and whatever good you do only affects them too.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: People cry out because of terrible persecution; they call for someone to save them from their oppressors.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: But no one asks, ‘Where is the God my maker, the one who gives songs in the night,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: who teaches us more than the animals, and makes us wiser than the birds?’
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: When they call out for help, God doesn't answer them because they are proud and evil people.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: God doesn't listen to their empty cries; [^239] the Almighty doesn't pay them any attention.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: How much less will God hear you when you say he can't see you?[^240] Your case is before him, so you have to wait for him.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: You're saying[^241] that God doesn't punish people in his anger and pays little attention to sin.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: You, Job, are talking nonsense, making long speeches when you know nothing!
segments.speaker: Elihu
